picks.match: Parramatta Eels vs Manly Warringah Sea Eagles
picks.reasoning: Manly sit at the bottom of the target odds range at 1.41, suggesting the market has strong conviction in their superiority here. The Sea Eagles recent form reference — including a noted Round 27 outing — indicates they are active and competitive late in the season, while Parramatta's odds of 3.35 reflect a side the market views as significantly outmatched. With news material pointing to Manly's continued activity and Parramatta showing no meaningful counterweight, this is the cleanest probability-backed selection across today's card.

picks.match: St George Illawarra Dragons vs Wests Tigers
picks.reasoning: Derby games are always tight but the Dragons at home at 2.36 is decent value against a Tigers side that's been inconsistent all year. Home ground advantage in a local rivalry counts for heaps, and I reckon the Dragons are due a big performance here. Gut says back the red V.

Ghana at nearly 10-to-1 on the World Cup stage? I'm practically PRINTING money. The Black Stars have a history of shocking South American heavyweights on the biggest stage, and Colombia — despite the short odds — are beatable when their attackers go cold. One moment of African magic and we're sipping something expensive.
